text: Prolonged estrus cycle and resulting decreased fertility have been studied in laboratory mammals by addition of various forms of estradiol and toxicants.  Prolonged estrus results in a failure to ovulate, inability to successfully reproduce, and decreased fertility.  Absence of a corpus luteum is sometimes used as an indicator for loss of fertility as the progesterone produced by the corpus luteum is necessary for implantation in the uterus and for maintaining an early pregnancy (Duncan 2021).
